title: Persona.js brings WebMCP-native agent UI to vanilla JavaScript
friendlyTitle: Persona helps websites add safer AI agents
summary: Persona is a lightweight VanillaJS library for building agentic web interfaces that can expose page actions as WebMCP tools. It supports tool approvals, carts, bookings, forms, voice, artifacts, theming, and streaming over any SSE backend.
friendlySummary: Persona is a JavaScript library for adding AI agent interfaces to websites. It can let agents use page actions, while still supporting user approval.
whyItMatters: As agents move into applications, the front-end pattern matters: users need visibility, approval, and clear controls. Libraries like Persona make it easier to add those agent behaviors without rebuilding an entire app stack.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can help developers build AI features that feel useful and controlled. Users can see what the agent is doing instead of trusting a hidden process.
